ideasThisWeek: Building content systems with AI. The gap between validating an idea and building the wrong thing. Why most founders skip the conversation stage.
newHooks: What if the problem you think you are solving is not the problem at all

settings.toneOfVoice: Direct, founder-native, honest. No fluff or corporate speak. Short punchy sentences. Rhetorical questions. Speak like someone who has been in the trenches not a polished presenter. Ground everything in specifics and real examples.
settings.generalThemes: Startup validation, building with AI, founder mindset, idea testing, removing friction from business. Target audience: early-stage founders and aspiring entrepreneurs.
settings.hooks:
Nobody talks about this but...
I made this work in 30 days by doing one thing...
Stop doing X if you want Y
The reason you are not getting results is...
This is the biggest mistake I see founders making
You are thinking about this completely wrong
Every founder needs to hear this
If I had to start over I would do this first
settings.exampleScripts:
EXAMPLE 1:
Okay so I just had the most uncomfortable call with a potential customer. They told me everything I built was solving the wrong problem. And honestly? Best call I had in months. Because now I know exactly what to fix. Stop avoiding the hard conversations.

EXAMPLE 2:
Three things I learned validating my last idea. One - your first 10 customers will tell you everything if you actually listen. Two - the problem they describe and the problem they have are almost never the same thing. Three - if they will not pay for it now they will not pay for it later.
